_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of embedded analytics and augmented analytics concepts, including AI-assisted insight generation, natural language querying, automated anomaly detection, and their impact on self-service BI adoption.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI AB domain. Knowledge AB-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-002 (TF, cosine: 0.527), AB-K-003 (AB, cosine: 0.520).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.895 (P=0.940, R=0.853). P exceeds R by 0.087 — the NICE element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of analytics" and "Knowledge of embedded analytics and augmented analytics concepts, including AI-a" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.544 < STS 0.660 (gap -0.116). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [] and [adoption, ai, anomaly] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.0%. Shared tokens: [analytics, knowledge]. NICE-only: []. WIDAI-only: [adoption, ai, anomaly, assisted, augmented, automated].
